title: Sample
text:
pacman::p_load(tidyverse, readxl, haven, sjlabelled, knitr) 
clean_sample <- read_sav("data/cleaned_data.sav")
vars_to_factor <- c( "A50", paste0("A51_", 1:7), "A52", paste0("A76_", 1:9), "A88", paste0("Q138_", 1:7) )


response_levels <- c(
  "Rarely or never",
  "Around once a month",
  "Around once a week",
  "Several times a week",
  "Most days"
)

item_labels <- c(
  A51_1 = "Headache",
  A51_2 = "Stomach ache",
  A51_3 = "Back ache",
  A51_4 = "Feeling low, depressed",
  A51_5 = "Irritable, bad-tempered",
  A51_6 = "Nervous",
  A51_7 = "Difficulty getting to sleep"
)

clean_sample %>%
  select(A51_1:A51_7) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = everything(),
    names_to = "item",
    values_to = "response"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    item = recode(item, !!!item_labels),
    item = factor(item, levels = unname(item_labels)),
    response = factor(
      response,
      levels = 1:5,
      labels = response_levels
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(response)) %>%
  count(item, response) %>%
  group_by(item) %>%
  mutate(percent = n / sum(n) * 100) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = item, y = percent, fill = response)) +
  geom_col() +
  labs(
    title = "Frequency of health complaints",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Percent",
    fill = "Response"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )

title: Welcome
text:
This is to show how websites can be built using R for a course.
First, have a look at this fluffy dog taken from Wikipedia.

title: About the sample
text:
Here we use fictitious data from 500 participants. Let’s start by looking at their weight and height!
The mean height is 170.89 cm. The tallest participant is 195 cm, while the smallest is 140 cm.
The mean weight is 67.64 kg. The largest participant is 135 kg, while the lightest is 30 kg.
Here is how their distribution looks:
